person.name: Joan Carminow
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography
Birth and Parents«/b»
Joan Carminow was the daughter of Sir Roger Carminow and his wife Joan Dinham.

Estimate here birth year as, say, 1290. Her husband was born about 1288. They were married when he was 5 in 1293.

«b»Marriage to Sir William de Walesbreu«/b»
In 1293 when he was 5 years old, she was married to William de Walesbreu the son of Marcus de Whalesborugh and his wife Matilda Arundel. 

Sir William de Walesbreu was born c1288 as he was age 14 in 1302 when he was heir to his older brother John who d.s.p. He was of Whalesborough (parish of St. Mawnan), Perran-Uthno (Udnow), Trerose, Langkerf (Lancarffe), Lamelwen (Lamellion), Elwen, and Tresider, in Cornwall. 

In 1302, William Bottreux brought a Writ of Wardship against Roger Carminow to regain possession of William, the brother and heir of John de Whalesboro, presumably because he had suddenly became more valuable. Roger replied to the writ, "William whilst your ward married our daughter." Bottreux was apparently arguing the marriage wasn't valid because "William de Whalesboro was only 5 years old then, and under 14 now". That they remained married is confirmed by a fine in 1319 which names William De Whalebreus and Johanna his wife as default remainder heirs of Oliver Carminow. In a fine of 10 May 1321, Joan, wife of William de Walesbreu was called a sibling of Oliver de Carminow and in another fine of 13 Oct 1333 she is called Joan, who was the wife of William de Walesbreu. As a result of this marriage, the Whalesborough's are said by the History of Trerose Manor website to have acquired the manor of Trerose which had been a Carminow manor; Joan's father Sir Roger Carminow (d. 1308) began the building of Trerose church and his monumental effigy can still be seen in St. Mawgan. However, the Whaleborough's certainly had an interest in Trerose as early as 1213 when it is part of the dower Osemunda.

«b»1328 Husband's Death«/b»
Sir William De Walesbreu died in 21 August 1328. Tristram Risdon says that "Willelmus Whalisburgh de Whalesburgh, knight, obitt 1328" bore the arms "Gules, three bandlets argent on a bordure sable, nine bezants."

«b»1335 Lawsuits and Presentations«/b»
"Joan, who was wife of William de Walesbrew" occurs in a suit in 1335. As Joan, relict of William de Whalesbreu, she presented to St. Mawnan-in-Kerrier 23 Feb 1347 and 6 Nov 1348; her son Sir John de Whalesbreu made the next presentation on 6 Aug 1349 and 3 April 1350.

«b»1362 Death«/b»
She died after 1 March 1361/62 as she presented one last time at St. Mawnan as Joan, relict of William de Whalesbreu.

«b»Issue«/b»
1.) John Whalesborough.

2.) Isabel Whalesborough. Isabel married twice to 1st Henry De Bodrugan and 2nd Henry Wilington.

3.) Mary Whalesborough married Thomas Chiverton.

zh.Bodmin, Cornwall, England, United Kingdo: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾博德明
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Carminow於1270年出生在英國可瓦爾可洛萬時，她的父親Roger Carminow31歲，母親Joanna Dinham18歲。她於1309年之前與Sir William de Whalesburgh結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年8月6日在英國英格蘭可瓦爾享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Carminow于1270年出生在英国可瓦爾可洛萬时，她的父亲Roger Carminow31岁，母亲Joanna Dinham18岁。她于1309年之前与Sir William de Whalesburgh结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1349年8月6日在英国英格兰可瓦爾享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Carminow was born in 1270, in Crowan, Cornwall, England, her father, Roger Carminow, was 31 and her mother, Joanna Dinham, was 18. She married Sir William de Whalesburgh before 1309. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 6 August 1349, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Carminow est née en 1270, à Crowan, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Roger Carminow, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Joanna Dinham, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Whalesburgh avant 1309. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 août 1349, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Carminow 1270 in Crowan, Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Carminow 31 und ihre Mutter Joanna Dinham 18. Sie hat vor 1309 Sir William de Whalesburgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. August 1349 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Carminow è nata il nel 1270 nel Crowan, Cornwall, England, sua padre, Roger Carminow, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Joanna Dinham, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de Whalesburgh prima del 1309. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 6 agosto 1349, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Carminowが1270年にイギリスコルンワルクロワンで生まれた時，父Roger Carminowは31歳，母Joanna Dinhamは18歳であった。1309年以前にSir William de Whalesburghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1349年8月6日にイギリスイギリスコルンワルで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Carminow(이)가 1270년 영국 코른월 크로원에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Carminow(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanna Dinham(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1309년 이전 Sir William de Whalesburgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 8월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Carminow родилась в 1270 г. в местности кроуань, корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Carminow было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Joanna Dinham, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Whalesburgh перед 1309 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 6 августа 1349 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Carminow nació en 1270, en Crowan, Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Carminow, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Joanna Dinham, tenía 18 años.
